methodsAttempted.notes: Maintenance HTTP scan detected content hash change and flagged as content_policy_delta.

OpenClaw agent fetched and compared the live page to the May 2026 snapshot. The URL now includes /ai/ subdirectory. The Follow Existing Tufts Policies subsection listing specific university policies (Standards of Professional Conduct, OVPE Usage Guidelines, academic integrity policies, research ethics/IRB, student conduct policies) has been removed from the Using AI Responsibly section.

artifacts.summary: Maintenance re-check of https://it.tufts.edu/guidance-using-ai-tufts detected a content_policy_delta: the URL now permanently redirects to /ai/ subdirectory, and the Follow Existing Tufts Policies subsection was removed from the page. Other sections (Choosing an AI Tool, Using AI Responsibly, Data Protection, Additional Resources) remain substantively intact. No new sources were discovered.

targets.sourceTitle: Guidance for Using AI at Tufts

stopConditions: Single-source maintenance delta review. No full re-crawl needed.

Focus only on the Follow Existing Tufts Policies section removal and URL change.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Using AI at Tufts

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for Using AI at Tufts
artifacts.diffNotes: Compared to snapshot from 2026-05-24: (1) URL now redirects from /guidance-using-ai-tufts to /ai/guidance-using-ai-tufts. (2) The Follow Existing Tufts Policies subsection under Using AI Responsibly has been removed; this subsection previously cross-referenced Standards of Professional Conduct, OVPE guidelines, academic integrity policies, research ethics/IRB requirements, and student conduct policies. (3) Several in-text hyperlinks have been added. (4) Remaining section structure and substantive guidance content are unchanged.

artifacts.claimText: Tufts Technology Services states that faculty and staff must not use unapproved AI tools for Tufts business, instruction, research, or administration without review and approval, especially when institutional data is involved.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Using AI at Tufts
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Tufts Technology Services states that, unless an AI tool has been explicitly approved for the data type, users must not enter restricted or regulated data, confidential or proprietary institutional data, or contract-restricted or licensed content into AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for Using AI at Tufts
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Tufts Technology Services states that users are responsible for all work, decisions, and outputs that involve AI, regardless of the tool used, and that AI does not replace professional or academic judgment, scholarly responsibility, or ethical obligations.

This removal may reduce the visibility of specific institutional policy cross-references within the TTS guidance page.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unless an AI tool has been explicitly approved for the data type, you must not enter: Restricted or regulated data, including: Student education records (FERPA); Health or clinical information (HIPAA); Research participant or human-subjects' data; Personnel, HR, or payroll information; Legal, disciplinary, or security-sensitive information; Confidential or proprietary institutional data; Contract-restricted or licensed content.

artifacts.title: Tufts University AI Policy Lightweight Maintenance Review
artifacts.summary: Lightweight maintenance review of https://it.tufts.edu/guidance-using-ai-tufts (now redirects to /ai/guidance-using-ai-tufts). The source-health scan flagged a content_policy_delta. Comparison with the May 2026 baseline snapshot confirmed a genuine but minor policy-content change: the Follow Existing Tufts Policies subsection was removed from the Using AI Responsibly section. This subsection previously cross-referenced Standards of Professional Conduct, OVPE Generative AI Usage Guidelines, academic integrity policies, research ethics/IRB requirements, and student conduct policies. The remaining sections are substantively intact. New in-text hyperlinks were added. Existing claims for unapproved-tool review, restricted-data prohibition, and accountability remain valid. A new observation claim documents the section removal. The core institutional AI guidance at Tufts is unchanged.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This is a lightweight maintenance review focused on the single changed source. Other Tufts sources (OVPE guidelines, CELT syllabus guidance, generative-ai-tools page) were not re-fetched.
The TTS approved-tools list can change and should be rechecked before publication.
Removal of a cross-reference section does not imply the referenced policies are no longer in effect — they remain independently accessible at their own URLs.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
